differences.title: 1. PARADIGM
differences.leftDesc: Events are immutable, append-only log. Consumers track their own position.
differences.rightDesc: Messages are delivered to consumers and removed. Supports routing.

differences.title: 2. CONSUMPTION
differences.leftDesc: Consumers poll for new messages. Higher throughput, lower latency.
differences.rightDesc: Broker pushes messages to consumers. Simpler for some apps.

differences.title: 3. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Designed for massive scale, high-volume real-time data feeds.
differences.rightDesc: Scales well for many tasks, excellent message routing.

differences.title: 4. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Activity tracking, log aggregation, stream processing, real-time analytics.
differences.rightDesc: Microservice communication, background job processing, asynchronous tasks.

differences.title: 5. RETENTION
differences.leftDesc: Messages retained based on time or size policies.

differences.title: 6. GUARANTEES
differences.leftDesc: Ensures messages are processed at least once, duplicates possible.
differences.rightDesc: Ensures messages are processed at most once, no duplicates.

differences.title: 7. REPLAYABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Consumers can re-read historical data from the log.
